families.nativeDecisionShape: practical third-street bring-in to completion or defend heuristics
families.repoReadyScope: reference-only practical coverage: stud-high third-street heuristics over 2 nodes

preview.heading: Practical heuristic preview
preview.copy: practical family では article-derived heuristic を scenario と推奨アクション単位で表示しています。

rows.title: Early positions need full three-card quality; late unopened spots can lean more on the up card
rows.body: Action: tighten_early_opening_requirements / The source makes position the first filter for whether a stud-high starter is playable or merely cosmetic.
rows.note: Applies: Third-street stud-high starting hands before the bring-in is heavily contested / Adjust: In early positions all three cards matter and the opening bar should be tighter

rows.title: Rolled-up third street start
rows.body: Action: raise_and_reraise_aggressively / The source calls rolled-up the best starting hand in Stud and explicitly recommends piling money in.
rows.note: Applies: Your first three cards are three-of-a-kind on third street

rows.title: Good stud draw starters stay playable only while their outs are live
rows.body: Action: play_good_pairs_flushes_or_connectors_when_outs_are_live / The source explicitly ties these starters to how many suit or straight outs are visible in other players' up cards.
rows.note: Applies: High pair, concealed pair, three-flush, or three-straight style starter / Adjust: Downgrade or fold flush and straight draws when multiple relevant outs are already exposed

rows.title: Three broadway-style high cards are playable unopened but fold to heavy third-street strength
rows.body: Action: complete_unopened_fold_vs_completion_and_raise / The source gives an explicit three-high-card example with opposite advice depending on prior third-street aggression.
rows.note: Applies: Three high cards such as A-K down with J up in an unopened pot / Adjust: Fold when facing both a completion and a raise

rows.title: Continue a third-street representation bluff mainly in heads-up pots when villain bricks fourth street
rows.body: Action: continue_bluff_on_fourth_street_when_villain_bricks / The source explicitly anchors bluff continuation to player count and how well the new up card connects with the opponent's visible structure.
rows.note: Applies: You completed or raised on third street to represent strength / Adjust: Give up more often once two or more players continue unless your own hand improves considerably

rows.title: As the heads-up aggressor, pressure visible brick cards
rows.body: Action: bet_again_when_opponent_catches_uncoordinated_upcard / The source closes by stating that heads-up aggressors can often win simply by betting when the opponent's new up card appears to miss.
rows.note: Applies: Heads-up stud pot after you showed aggression earlier in the hand / Adjust: Do not auto-fire when the new up card obviously strengthens their visible pair, draw, or made-hand line

families.nativeDecisionShape: practical third-street low-door completion or defend heuristics
families.repoReadyScope: reference-only practical coverage: razz third-street heuristics over 1 node

preview.heading: Practical heuristic preview
preview.copy: practical family では article-derived heuristic を scenario と推奨アクション単位で表示しています。

rows.title: Open wider when action folds around toward the right of the bring-in
rows.body: Action: tighten_immediately_left_loosen_when_folds_reach_you / The source explicitly maps Razz starting-hand quality to how many players remain between you and the bring-in.
rows.note: Applies: Comparing seats immediately left of the bring-in against later seats after folds / Adjust: Immediately left of the bring-in needs a materially stronger start

rows.title: Five-high or better razz starts are premium; six-high is still comfortably playable
rows.body: Action: raise_or_reraise_with_five_high_or_better / The source gives explicit practical thresholds at five-high, six-high, and seven-high.
rows.note: Applies: Unpaired Razz start whose highest card is five or lower / Adjust: Six-high starts are usually still playable even from early position

rows.title: Use visible low-card blockers before continuing with 4-5 down, 7 up style starts
rows.body: Action: play_only_when_low_outs_are_mostly_live / The source gives concrete blocker thresholds instead of a vague 'be careful with dead cards' warning.
rows.note: Applies: Early-position hand similar to 4-5 down with 7 up / Adjust: Two exposed blockers makes the spot questionable

rows.title: Visible opponent cards matter at least as much as your own cards
rows.body: Action: audit_upcards_before_continuing / The source frames visible-card tracking as the difference between losing and winning Razz players.
rows.note: Applies: Any Razz continuation or opening decision where live low cards determine hand quality / Adjust: Do not evaluate your hand in isolation from opponents' up cards

families.nativeDecisionShape: practical third-street split-pot completion or defend heuristics
families.repoReadyScope: reference-only practical coverage: stud8 third-street heuristics over 2 nodes

preview.heading: Practical heuristic preview
preview.copy: practical family では article-derived heuristic を scenario と推奨アクション単位で表示しています。

rows.title: Avoid 9-T-J style starts that are trapped between the high and low halves
rows.body: Action: downgrade_or_fold_middle_card_starts_without_strong_backup / The source explicitly calls these cards 'no man's land' because they miss both halves of the game too often.
rows.note: Applies: Third-street Stud Hi/Lo starts built around Nines, Tens, or many Jacks / Adjust: Treat these cards as low-disqualified and usually too weak to own the high half by themselves

rows.title: Ace-plus-wheel starts are premium split-pot starters
rows.body: Action: play_aggressively_with_ace_low_three_card_starts / The source gives A-5 down with 3 up as a very good example because it can win both halves.
rows.note: Applies: Start contains an Ace plus supporting wheel cards, such as A-5 down with 3 up / Adjust: Value rises when the hand also carries concealed high backup like an Ace or flush potential

rows.title: Do not auto-play every three-to-a-low start; run the checklist first
rows.body: Action: evaluate_low_quality_players_and_dead_outs_before_continue / The source explicitly turns this family into a three-question decision process rather than an automatic continue.
rows.note: Applies: Holding three to a low on third street in Stud Hi/Lo / Adjust: Ask how low the draw is

rows.title: Weak eight-low draws should usually fold when real low competition or dead outs are visible
rows.body: Action: fold_weak_eight_low_draws_when_low_competition_exists / The source gives a concrete weak eight-low example and tells you to pivot based on whether the low side is contested.
rows.note: Applies: Weak three-to-a-low hand like 8-6 down with 4 up / Adjust: Fold more often when low cards such as A, 2, 3, or 5 are showing in other hands

rows.title: Once you likely own the low half, pressure high-only opponents aggressively
rows.body: Action: bet_and_raise_made_lows_aggressively / The source repeatedly recommends pushing money in when you are freerolling the low half and others are contesting only the high side.
rows.note: Applies: You have a made low and are likely the only qualifying low at showdown / Adjust: Avoid being the high-only caller against an aggressive made-low player unless you are very sure of the high half

families.nativeDecisionShape: practical round-aware triple-draw open or defend heuristics
families.repoReadyScope: reference-only practical coverage: triple-draw heuristics over 2 nodes

preview.heading: Practical heuristic preview
preview.copy: practical family では article-derived heuristic を scenario と推奨アクション単位で表示しています。

rows.title: Early position wants a pat seven-or-eight or a hand that is one card away from strong
rows.body: Action: open_early_only_with_pat_or_near_pat_strength / The source explicitly says early position should start from a pat seven/eight or a hand that is one card away from strong.
rows.note: Applies: Opening round in early position before the first draw in 2-7 Triple Draw / Adjust: Treat discard-two opens as rare exceptions rather than default continues

rows.title: Early-position discard-two raises are limited to wheel-heavy starts such as 234 through 238
rows.body: Action: raise_only_whitelisted_discard_two_starts_from_early_position / The source gives an explicit early-position whitelist instead of a vague preference for smooth discard-two hands.
rows.note: Applies: You would need to discard two cards from early position on the opening round / Adjust: Hands outside 234, 235, 236, 237, or 238 should generally not be raised from early position

rows.title: When folded to the button, late-position pressure can override raw card quality
rows.body: Action: raise_very_wide_when_folded_to_button / The source states that button opens can be raised regardless of cards because the blinds fold often enough.
rows.note: Applies: Action folds to you on the button in a late-position opening spot / Adjust: Expect blinds to fold often enough that immediate fold equity is part of the line

rows.title: With position, decide whether a nine low stands pat by watching how many cards villain draws
rows.body: Action: stand_pat_vs_multi_card_draw_break_nine_vs_one_card_draw / The source uses a nine-low example to show that draw-count visibility should directly change the keep-or-break decision.
rows.note: Applies: You hold a rough pat nine such as 9-7-5-3-2 and act after seeing the opponent draw / Adjust: Consider standing pat when the opponent draws two or more cards

rows.title: Out of position in one-card versus one-card final-round spots, polarize between strong hands and total misses
rows.body: Action: bet_strong_hands_and_garbage_check_call_middle_showdown_value / The source gives an explicit final-round polar strategy instead of a generic reminder to bluff sometimes.
rows.note: Applies: Heads-up or near-heads-up third-draw spot out of position / Adjust: Bet total garbage that cannot win at showdown

rows.title: Against super-passive opponents, widen the bluff bucket and shrink the check-call bucket
rows.body: Action: increase_bluff_frequency_reduce_passive_bluff_catches / The source explicitly carves out a passive-opponent adjustment rather than leaving the final-round plan static.
rows.note: Applies: Final betting round in one-card versus one-card spots against a very passive opponent / Adjust: If villain rarely bets without a monster, convert more weak showdown hands into bluffs
